refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: to support achieving a defined cycle time target for a specific operational cycle. It is already focused on what the system must do (support achieving the cycle time target) without specifying how, thus it is solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and verify that the 95th percentile (p95) cycle time is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load handling mechanism to minimize load time
Optimize travel path and speed control to minimize travel time
Optimize dump mechanism to minimize dump time
Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time
Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time performance feedback
selected_dp.selected_dp: Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time

potential_sources: Interdependence between load, travel, and dump operations timing
Shared control logic for cycle coordination
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically integrates the three sub-operations into a coordinated cycle, which supports independence of individual operation optimizations while enabling overall cycle time minimization. Potential coupling arises from the coordination logic but is necessary to meet the cycle time target.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that load, travel, and dump operations can be independently optimized before coordination
Risk that coordination complexity may introduce delays if not well designed
Assumes site conditions and duty cycle remain within expected parameters
Risk that safety policies may constrain operation speeds affecting cycle time
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining DPs for each sub-operation (load, travel, dump) and a separate DP for their coordination to clarify boundaries and support modular optimization and verification.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Coordinates load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time across the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchange status and progress data with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Send and receive commands related to operation sequencing and coordination

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicate with external systems and remote operators

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as it encompasses overall coordination logic; minimal diffusion as coordination is centralized here.
Vehicle module handles physical actions but coordination logic would be diffused across other modules.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, not overall coordination; would cause high diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety, not operation coordination; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the coordination DP because it is designed to manage system-wide operational sequencing and optimization, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution may grow as it handles multiple coordination tasks.
Dependency on accurate and timely data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may introduce coupling risks.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If coordination logic becomes too complex or tightly coupled with vehicle control, consider decomposing coordination into submodules within NGHE Solution to maintain modularity without leaking functionality outside.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying coordination of load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time, directly supporting achievement of the customer-defined cycle time target.

diffusion_risks: Dependency on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for data may introduce coupling risks, but coordination logic is centralized within the NGHE Solution module, minimizing diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion and appropriate packaging of the coordination DP. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring the cycle time p95 against the customer-defined target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, as well as ensuring compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the coordination DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well-justified, with explicit interfaces defined for telemetry, tasking, and network link to support coordination activities.

recommended_revisions: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module as coordination logic expands; consider decomposing into submodules to maintain modularity and reduce coupling.
Ensure robust and timely data exchange mechanisms with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to mitigate coupling risks and maintain coordination effectiveness.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The NGHE Solution module demonstrates high containment and low diffusion, indicating appropriate architectural packaging. Although there are noted risks related to coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, these are manageable with monitoring and robust data exchange mechanisms. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and verified coordination module is essential, and further decomposition is not currently warranted to avoid unnecessary complexity.

revise.why: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module as coordination logic expands; consider decomposing into submodules if modularity or coupling issues arise.

assumptions: Coordination logic as currently defined sufficiently meets the customer cycle time target and safety requirements.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules remain stable and reliable.
open_risks: Dependency on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may introduce coupling risks.
Potential complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module over time.
evidence_needed: Performance data confirming cycle time p95 meets targets under operational conditions.
Verification results confirming compliance with safety plans and operational policies.
Monitoring reports on module complexity and coupling metrics.
